titel: Hearing and temporary transfer in arrest warrant cases
html:
The bill proposes amendments to the Act on surrender from Sweden under a European arrest warrant and the Act on surrender under a Nordic arrest warrant. The aim is to clarify Sweden's implementation and align it with the case law of the Court of Justice of the EU. The proposals include, among other things, the following.

• Where an arrest warrant concerns prosecution, the requested person shall, at the request of the issuing authority, either be able to be heard in the executing state or be temporarily transferred to the issuing state.

• Where the requested person has been protected by privileges or immunity in Sweden, the time limits in a surrender case shall be counted from the day the district court received information that these have ceased.

• Consent from the issuing state shall be required in order to refuse a surrender for the enforcement of a custodial sentence and instead enforce the sentence in Sweden.

The amendments are proposed to enter into force in 2027.
